description: Measures a candidate's ability to analyze, interpret, and draw logical conclusions from numerical data presented in tables, charts, and graphs. Essential for roles requiring data analysis and financial decisions.
Assesses logical problem-solving skills, abstract thinking, and the ability to identify patterns, relationships, and rules in sequences of shapes or symbols.
Evaluates the candidate's ability to apply logical rules, make deductions, and solve problems based on incomplete information or logical arguments. Critical for coding, legal, and analytical roles.
A comprehensive cognitive assessment combining numerical, deductive, and inductive reasoning tasks into a single test. Ideal for broad talent screening across multiple skills.
Evaluates the candidate's ability to read, comprehend, and interpret complex written passages, business reports, and instruction sets. Important for communication-heavy and research roles.

The industry standard for measuring workplace personality, style, and behavioral preferences. Assesses 32 key personality dimensions mapped directly to job competencies, leadership suitability, and culture fit.
Measures basic mathematical computation skills, speed, and accuracy in fundamental arithmetic operations. Used heavily for entry-level finance, clerk, and admin roles.
Assesses attention to detail, precision, and speed in comparing lists of data, strings, and alphanumeric codes. Ideal for quality control, administration, and data entry roles.
Measures understanding of physical and mechanical principles, forces, electrical circuits, and machinery operations. Best for engineers, technicians, and manual roles.
Evaluates how candidates respond to realistic, challenging workplace scenarios, decision-making conflicts, and professional dilemmas. Measures core workplace behavior and judgment.
Measures practical software engineering skills, syntax knowledge, and coding proficiency in the Java programming language. Includes debugging, algorithmic thinking, and object-oriented design.
Assesses C++ programming aptitude, syntax, debugging, and memory management. Evaluates the candidate's understanding of pointers, templates, and performance optimization.
Evaluates core programming skills, scripting ability, data structures, and standard library knowledge using Python. Suitable for software engineers, data analysts, and automation developers.

assessment_name: SHL Customer Service Situational Judgment Test
description: Designed specifically to assess communication, empathy, resilience, and problem-solving skills in customer support, retail, and client service environments.
Focuses on behavioral traits aligned with sales success, such as resilience, persuasion, achievement orientation, competitiveness, and client relationship management.
